methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $1.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Retro Biosciences is a Series A-stage biotech company founded by Joe Betts-LaCroix that has raised $180M to date. The valuation assessment triangulates against a comparable universe of 3 public biotech firms (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers in Biotech & Health, including Series B-stage companies such as Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), and Asimov ($800M).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using the $180M total raised as a proxy base value of $1.0B and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, implies a $2.0B valuation. The peer-median approach across 6 same-sector and adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $805M, with the peer set ranging from $100M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples cannot be applied because target revenue is not disclosed, though the 3-ticker public median P/S multiple is 7.8x.

The triangulated valuation range is $684M (low) to $2.3B (high), with a median of $1.4B. The dispersion between peer-median ($805M) and stage-progression ($2.0B) reflects material uncertainty around whether capital-raised-to-valuation multiples or forward-stage progression better model this company's position. Revenue visibility and clinical-stage progress would sharpen conviction on where within this range Retro's fair value lies.
